pi_items.source: setup_task.sh and zipfile parsing of the official OpenRocket sample

privileged_info_summary: Completed PI audit for openrocket_env mass_audit_ballast_optimization task. Confirmed that the source file is the real 'A simple model rocket' example by Sampo Niskanen, which is modified at runtime with a 120g Electronics Payload via setup_task.sh. All metadata path parameters and mass bounds match the setup and verifier scripts. Discovered a critical defect in the task's scoring logic: the original sample file natively contains a 'Wadding' mass component of 0.002 kg. The verifier incorrectly attributes this pre-existing component as agent-added ballast, automatically awarding 30 points (25 for adding mass, 5 for it being suspiciously light). Thus, an agent can achieve a passing score of at least 65/100 (surpassing the 60 threshold) simply by opening the file, re-running the simulation, and saving, without adding any actual compensating ballast.
